expected_properties: dout should reflect the memory content corresponding to each addr value presented in rapid succession
No glitches or undefined values should appear on dout during address transitions
additional_cases.goal: Verify that consecutive address changes are handled correctly without assuming a fixed latency
notes: Since the ROM is combinational, dout is expected to follow addr changes within the same observation window
stimulus_intent: Apply a sequence of different addr values back-to-back without idle cycles
timing_assumptions: Do not assume a fixed number of cycles; monitor dout after each addr change within a reset‑safe window

expected_properties: dout reflects the combinational value of mem[addr]
No dependence on clock or reset
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Verify combinational read behavior of the ROM
notes: The always @(*) block should produce dout immediately after addr changes
stimulus_intent: Drive a single legal addr value and observe dout
timing_assumptions: Observe dout within the same delta cycle as the addr change (reset‑safe observation)

expected_properties: dout correctly outputs mem[0x00] and mem[0xFF]
Behavior is consistent for minimum and maximum address values
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Exercise boundary addresses of the ROM
notes: Edge case includes the lowest (0x00) and highest (0xFF) address values
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like (0x00), one-like (0x01), and max (0xFF) patterns on addr
timing_assumptions: Do not assume fixed latency; monitor dout after each address stimulus within a reset‑safe window
